California
Fair POlitical
Practices Cornrnission
February 6, L992
Bradley w. Hertz
Attorney at Law
15223 ltagnolia BIvd., Suite A
Sherman Oaks, CA 91403
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. I-92-40
Dear Mr. Hertz 3
This is in response to your letter requesting advice under
the canpaign provisions of ti're Political R6fo.rn a6t 1"th" a"1rr11/
and coniirrns advice I gave you by telephone. Because your request
is a general inquiry, we treat it,as one for informal ass j-stance
pursuant to Regulation 18329 (c).tl
OUESTIONS
1. I,Iust the name of a controlled cornrnittee include the
nane of the controlling officeholder?
2. Must a committee primarily formed to oppose the recalL
of an elected official 
include in references to the
conmittee a statement that the cornmittee i's opposed
to the recall?
3. Is the Form 419 the correct campaign disclosure form to
be fiLed by a controlled comrnittee primaril-y formed to
oppose a recall?
L/ Governnent Code Sections SLooo-91015.- Al-1 statutory
references are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated.
cornrnission regulations appear at 2 california code of ReguLations
Section L8OO0; et seo. e11 references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 5 of the California code of Regulations'
2/ Inf orrnal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
i-rnmunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice'
(Section 83114; Regufation 18329(c) (3) . )
-[28 J Strccr, Srritc SOr't a P.(). I]or liO? o Slcr:rmcnr,'' !'.-\ ()5SO+-OS(r; . (91(r) ]ll-iot'tt


Bradley Hertz
t-92- 40
Page 2
CONCLUSIONS
l-. A controll.ed comrnittee is not required to include the
name of the controlling officeholder as part of the
name of the conmittee. Ho$rever, a controlling candi-
date's name nust be included in the identification 
of
the sender on all mass mailings sent by the committee.
2. A committee primarj.ly formed to oppose the recalI of an
elected off icj.al is required to include in any reference
to the committee required by Iaw a statement that the
committee is opposed to the teca1I.
3. The Forn 419 is the correct carnpaign disclosure form to
be filed by a committee prirnariJ.y formed to oppose a
recall, including such cornmittees that are controlled
by a candidate or officeholder.
ANALYSI S
In June L988, the voters of california adopted Proposition 68
which amended the Act. one of the provisions of Proposition 68
was a requj-rement that the name of a comrnittee shall include or be
accompanied by the name of any person by which the conmj-ttee is
controLled. on November 1, 1990, the california supreme court
issued its decision in Taxpavers to Lirnit Campaiqn Spendinq v.
Fair PoLitical Practices Comrnission (1990) 5l- cal.3d 744, and this
decision became final on January 18, 1991. The Supreme Court held
that all of the provisions of Proposition 68 were invalid.
consequently, the Act does not include any requirement that
the name of a controlled corunittee must incLude the name of the
person who controls the cornrnittee, However. p)-ease note that the
Act does require a controll-ed committee that is the sender of a
mass mailing to include on the outside of the nailing the name of
the person controlling the committee, in addition to other
required information. (Section 84305. )
Your second question concerns the requirement for a ballot
measure comrnittee to identify the rneasure it supports or opposes.
section 84107 states:
I.lithin 30 days of the designation of the numerical
order of propositions appearing on the ballot, 
any
cornrnittee which has as its principal activity the
support of or opposition to a ballot measure' shall, if
supporting the measure, include the statement, rra
connittee f or Proposition _, 'r or, if opposing the
measure, include the statenent, "a committee against
Proposition _, " itt any reference to the committee
required b1, Iaw.


Bradley Hertz
r-92- 40
Page 3
The comnittee you represent is formed primarily to oppose the
recal-I of a local eLected officer. 
There is no ball-ot number or
letter trdesignationtr for the recalI measure. In this situation,
the committee $rill comply with Section 84107, and the purpose of
Section 841O7 qril1 be accomplished, by including the statement, rra
committee against the recall of (name of officeholder) ir in any
reference to the committee required by law.
In situations $rhere there is no Itdesignation of the numerical
order of propositions appearing on the ballot, " an issue arises
concerning the point in time at which Section 841-07 applies. As a
practical matter, it is suggested that the language quoted above
be included in the comnittee's name when the conmittee files its
Statement of Organization ( Forrn 410).
In the case of a committee supporting or opposing the
qualification of a measure which changes to a committee supporting
or opposing the passage of a measure, v/e suggest that the J-anguage
required by Section 84107 be incfuded in the connittee,s nane when
it arnends its Statement of organization to change the description
of the nature of its activi,ties.
In any event, srhen there is no ballot number or letter
designation for a measure, the language required by Section 84107
rnust be included i.n aII references to the committee required by
the Act commencing no later than 30 days after the date the
election is ca11ed (i.e., the date the governing body adopts a
resolution setting the election date).
Finally, you asked if Form 419 is the correct carnpaign
disclosure form to be filed by a conrnittee primarily forrned to
oppose the recaLL of an elected offj-ciaI. 
The Act defines
ttmeasure'r to include a recall-, 
(Section A2O43. ) Consequently,
Form 419--BaIIot Measure committee Campaign Statement, is the
correct forn to be used by the cornrnittee.
If you have additional questions, please contact rne at (915)
322-5662.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral Counsel,
,t
c ntl'^- L L/a',t d-(':-c')
By: Carla Wardlow
Assistant Division chief
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Div j-sion
